query: USMCA joint review July 1 2026 outcome - did US provide written notice of withdrawal under Article 34.6? Latest news on Trump USMCA withdrawal or renewal decision June-July 2026
answer:
## Summary

**The US did NOT provide written notice of withdrawal under Article 34.6.** At the mandatory July 1, 2026 joint review, the United States *declined to renew/extend* the USMCA (an Article 34.7 non-renewal), which is a legally distinct action from an Article 34.6 withdrawal.

**Key findings:**
- USTR Jamieson Greer's official statement: *"The United States did not agree to renew the USMCA in its current form. As a result, the USMCA is not renewed"* — but the Agreement **remains fully in force**.
- Mexico and Canada both supported the 16-year extension; the US was the sole party declining.
- Non-renewal triggered **Article 34.7.4**: mandatory **annual joint reviews** each year through the Agreement's scheduled expiration of **July 1, 2036**.
- The 16-year extension is **deferred, not lost** — it can still be adopted "at any time" via written confirmation by the three heads of government.
- All tariffs, rules of origin, and investment protections remain unchanged.
- **Next step:** third round of bilateral US–Mexico negotiations the week of **July 20, 2026** in Mexico City. US–Canada substantive negotiations had not yet begun.
- No source shows any Article 34.6 withdrawal notice; Brookings notes the parties *retain* that separate withdrawal option but did not exercise it.

This was the first-ever use of the USMCA review mechanism, and the analysis consistently frames it as a "10-year countdown" scenario rather than a withdrawal.

sources.title: Ambassador Greer Issues Statement on the USMCA Joint Review
sources.snippet: The United States did not agree to renew the USMCA in its current form. As a result, the USMCA is not renewed... the Agreement remains in force pending resolution of these issues or until the Agreement's termination. The United States will meet with Mexico the week of July 20 for a third round of bilateral negotiations.

sources.title: USMCA 2026 Joint Review: United States declines to extend Agreement, triggering annual reviews
sources.snippet: The US declined to confirm intention to extend the Agreement for an additional 16-year period... This decision triggers the annual joint review process under Article 34.7.4... The 16-year extension is deferred, not lost. Agreement remains in full force. Next bilateral round with Mexico week of July 20 in Mexico City.

sources.title: U.S. Declines to Renew the USMCA/T-MEC/CUSMA, Setting Stage for Annual Review Cycles
sources.snippet: The US decision does not terminate the Agreement. Rather, it triggers the Agreement's annual review mechanism... scheduled expiration in July 2036. Canada and Mexico requested clarity from USTR on a proposed timeline; the US has yet to outline the structure.

sources.title: USMCA review reviewed: Lessons from the first use of USMCA's review mechanism
sources.snippet: If one or more governments decline to support an extension, the agreement does not immediately expire... the parties also retain the ability to amend the agreement by mutual consent under Article 34.3 or to withdraw separately under Article 34.6.

sources.title: USMCA joint review: Key updates for North American trade
sources.snippet: Not renewed during its required joint review on July 1, 2026, the agreement will remain in force through its scheduled expiration on July 1, 2036. First annual joint review begins July 1, 2027; bilateral US-Mexico negotiations week of July 20.

sources.title: Trump Administration Decides Against Renewing USMCA, Opts for Annual Review Process
sources.snippet: On July 1, the United States formally declined to renew the USMCA, triggering an annual review process that extends negotiations for up to a decade.

sources.title: Mexico Enters New Phase: USMCA Continuity and Annual Review with the U.S. and Canada
sources.snippet: The July 1, 2026 trilateral joint review confirmed no consensus among the three countries to automatically extend the agreement for an additional 16 years. Does not terminate the agreement.

sources.title: U.S. Decides Not to Renew USMCA, Triggers Annual Reviews
sources.snippet: The United States decided on Wednesday not to renew its trade agreement with Canada and Mexico; White House hopes annual reviews will curb the US trade deficit.

sources.title: USMCA Chapter 34 Final Provisions (Articles 34.6 Withdrawal, 34.7 Review and Term Extension)
sources.snippet: Article 34.6 Withdrawal provides a party may withdraw on six months' written notice; Article 34.7 governs review and 16-year term extension.

data_points.metric: USMCA joint review meeting date (six-year mandatory review)
US Article 34.6 written withdrawal notices filed as of July 2026
USMCA scheduled term expiration date (absent extension)
Additional term length available via extension under Article 34.7
Parties confirming support for 16-year extension (Mexico and Canada)
First annual joint review start date (post non-renewal)
USTR reporting deadline to Congress before each annual review
